specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from acceptable agencies (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must display credit scores from all three bureaus with minimum qualifying score of 700
Must show complete tradeline details including payment history, balances, and credit limits
Must include mortgage/housing history if reported
Must show inquiries within the last 90-120 days
Must include public record searches for each city where borrower resided in last 2 years
Must identify any collections, charge-offs, disputes, or derogatory items
Must be dated within 90 days of loan application
Must verify Social Security number for all borrowers
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate Flex Supreme program eligibility and pricing at 700 FICO
Tradeline requirements must be verified: 3 tradelines reporting 12+ months OR 2 tradelines reporting 24+ months
Credit report is primary source to identify any credit events (BK, FC, SS, DIL) and verify seasoning requirements
Housing history must be verified to confirm 0x30x12 mortgage payment history if applicable
Public records search confirms no undisclosed judgments or liens
Inquiries must be reviewed to identify potential undisclosed new debt
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Guidelines - Credit Report section requires tri-merge RMCR for all borrowers

specifications: Must provide statements for most recent 2 consecutive months
Must include all pages of each statement
Must show borrower/account holder name matching application
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or masked)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must cover all accounts intended for funds to close and reserves
Large deposits exceeding 50% of qualifying income require sourcing documentation
Must show sufficient liquid assets to cover required reserve months
Must calculate reserves based on PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must use verified account balances from acceptable asset sources
Must show reserves remain after funds to close are deducted
Reserve assets must be in borrower name or joint with spouse
Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close per NQMF guidelines
Down payment, closing costs, and prepaid items must be evidenced from acceptable sources
Reserve requirements must be supported by verified liquid assets
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower identity
Transaction history required to identify large deposits requiring additional documentation
Reserve requirements apply based on program, property type, and occupancy
Reserves provide evidence of borrower's ability to sustain mortgage payments
NQMF guidelines require verification of assets for reserves
Reserve calculation must reconcile with bank statement balances net of funds to close
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date

specifications: Must be current, unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must show borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photo, date of birth, and expiration date
Acceptable forms: Driver's License, State ID, U.S. Passport, Permanent Resident Card

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification is required before credit, title, and compliance documents can be validated
Name on ID must match loan application and all borrower-signed documents

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct tax years (most recent 1-2 years as required by income type)
Must list correct borrower SSN and current address
Must authorize IRS to release tax transcripts to lender

reasons_needed.reason: Guidelines require a fully executed and signed IRS Form-4506C for income verification
Tax transcript authorization required to validate income documentation and detect fraud

specifications: Must cover most recent 12-month period at minimum
Must identify borrower name and property address
Must show payment due dates and actual payment dates
Must indicate any late payments with severity (30/60/90/120 days)
Must be from current mortgage servicer or reflected on credit report
Must include all mortgages held by borrower (including investment properties)
Must show current loan balance and payment amount
Must indicate if any modifications, forbearances, or payment deferrals occurred
reasons_needed: Housing history verification is required for all programs per NQM guidelines
Mortgage payment history must demonstrate 0x30x12 standard for tradeline qualification
Any mortgage lates within lookback period affect program eligibility and pricing
Modification or forbearance history must be disclosed and seasoning verified
Investment property borrower likely has existing mortgage requiring history verification
120-day mortgage late constitutes a Housing Event requiring additional seasoning
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Guidelines - Housing History section and Standard Tradelines section

specifications: Must be most recent 30 days of paystubs
Must be typed or computer generated
Must show borrower's full name and address
Must show employer name
Must show year-to-date earnings
Must show rate of pay
Must separate base, overtime, bonus, or commission earnings if applicable
If garnishments or loan deductions are present, additional documentation will be required
reasons_needed: Full Documentation income verification requires 30 days of paystubs per NQM guidelines
YTD earnings are required to calculate qualifying income
Current employment and income stability must be verified
Variable income components require separate review for averaging
document_requests.guideline_reference: FULL DOCUMENTATION - Income must be verified with 30 days of paystubs

specifications: Must include W-2 forms covering most recent 1-2 year period
Must be typed or computer generated
Must show borrower's full name and address
Must show employer name
Must support income continuity and stability
Must reconcile to 1003 income declaration and paystub YTD
reasons_needed: Full Documentation income verification requires 1 or 2 years W-2 forms per NQM guidelines
Historical income documentation supports income stability analysis
W-2 income must reconcile with paystub and VOE for qualification

specifications: Must be completed by current employer
Must show borrower name and position/title
Must show employment start date
Must show current salary/hourly rate
Must show YTD earnings and prior year earnings
Must indicate probability of continued employment
Must be dated within 30 days of closing
If commission income, must cover most recent 2-year period
Must be from CPA, regulatory agency, licensing bureau, or Secretary of State
Must verify business has been in existence for minimum 2 years
Must confirm current year registration if from Secretary of State
Must be obtained within 30 business days of closing
Must support self-employment history of 2 consecutive years in same business
reasons_needed: Full Documentation requires electronic verification of employment or written VOE
Employment continuity and income stability must be independently verified
Commission earners require 2-year history via VOE per guideline requirements
Business existence must be independently verified through disinterested third party
Self-employment verification required within 30 business days of closing per guideline
Stability and continuance of self-employment must be documented
document_requests.guideline_reference: FULL DOCUMENTATION - electronic verification of employment

specifications: Must provide copy of earnest money check (front and back) showing cleared status
Must show EMD amount matching purchase contract
Must show funds sourced from verified borrower account
Must include deposit receipt or escrow confirmation if applicable
Must trace EMD funds to bank statement showing withdrawal
reasons_needed: EMD represents borrower contribution toward down payment
EMD source must be verified as acceptable funds
Cleared EMD confirms borrower commitment to transaction
EMD amount contributes to funds to close calculation
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Funds must be from acceptable sources

specifications: Must include donor's name, address, phone number, and relationship to borrower
Must state dollar amount of gift
Must include donor's signed statement that no repayment is expected or required
Donor must be relative by blood, marriage, adoption, or legal guardianship
Alternatively, donor may be domestic partner, fiancé, former relative, or godparent
Donor may not be builder, developer, real estate agent, or any interested party to transaction
For Flex Supreme Full Doc at 80% LTV or less: 100% gift funds eligible for down payment and closing costs
For LTV above 80%: minimum 5% borrower contribution required for owner-occupied
reasons_needed: Gift funds require documentation per NQMF Gift Funds guidelines
Donor relationship must be verified as eligible
No-repayment statement confirms funds are true gift, not loan
Gift may not come from interested party to the transaction

specifications: Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must identify property type and number of units
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date (or 365 days with Form 1004D recertification)
Must include all pages, addenda, and photographs
Must use FNMA Form 1004 for 1-unit properties, Form 1025 for 2-4 units, or Form 1073 for condos
Must include street map showing subject and comparable locations
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan
Must include color photographs of front, street, rear views, kitchen, bathrooms, main living area
Must include comparable sales analysis and appraiser certification
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, health/safety issues, or deferred maintenance
Must show property condition rating (C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must include appraiser license/certification number
Must comply with Appraiser Independence Requirements
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting per NQMF guidelines
LTV 75% eligibility depends on appraised value confirmation
Property type and unit count affect program eligibility
Property condition rating impacts loan eligibility (C5/C6 ineligible)
Subject property identity must be consistent across loan file
FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less required for appraisal review process

specifications: Must show SSR score from Fannie Mae submission
Must identify subject property address
Must include UCDP submission confirmation
SSR Score must be 2.5 or less for standard acceptance
Must include all findings and flags for underwriter review
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less
SSR validates appraisal quality and value reasonableness
Required secondary appraisal product to support transaction value

specifications: Must be issued by FEMA-certified vendor
Must identify subject property address
Must identify flood zone designation (A, V, X, etc.)
Must include Section B4 Flood Zone and Section B5 NFIP Map status
Must include Section D Determination confirming whether property is in SFHA
Must include FEMA Map Panel Number and effective date
Must be current and valid as of closing date
reasons_needed: Flood Certificate required per NQMF guidelines to determine if property is in SFHA
Determines whether flood insurance is required for loan
Properties in Zone A or V require FEMA Program participation
Appraisal report flood zone must be reconciled with flood certificate

specifications: Must be NFIP standard policy or private policy with equal coverage
Must name all persons holding title as named insured
Must include property address matching loan documents
Must include standard mortgagee clause (not loss payable clause)
Minimum coverage: lesser of 100% replacement cost, $250,000 NFIP max, or UPB
Must show policy number, effective and expiration dates
Must show premium amount and deductible
Must include cancellation notice requirements
Must be in force at closing and maintained throughout loan term
reasons_needed: Required per NQMF guidelines for properties in Special Flood Hazard Area (Zone A or V)
Federal flood insurance requirements mandate coverage for SFHA properties
Protects collateral value against flood damage

specifications: Must name all persons holding title as named insured
Must include property address matching loan documents
Must include standard mortgagee clause
Must provide coverage at replacement cost value
Must show policy number, effective and expiration dates
Must include cancellation notice provision requiring insurer notification to mortgagee
Must show premium amount and deductible
Must be in force at closing
reasons_needed: Required per NQMF guidelines to protect collateral
Insurance protects lender's security interest in property
Standard requirement for all mortgage transactions

specifications: Must be issued by a title insurer qualified to do business in California
Must identify all borrower/owner/vested parties by full legal name
Must identify subject property address and complete legal description
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer name and effective date
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF guidelines
Must indicate final title policy will be issued after funding
Must be dated within 90 days of closing
Must reconcile vesting with borrower names and transaction structure
Must conform to ALTA 2006 standard form or approved state equivalent
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership, lien position, and insurable interest
Vesting and subject property must align with loan file
Liens and exceptions may affect closing conditions and first lien position
24-month title history required to review for flipping activity or inconsistencies
Title exceptions must be reviewed for acceptability per NQMF guidelines
Title insurer must be qualified in California per state licensing requirements

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership of subject property
Must identify all vested owners by full legal name
Must include complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show manner of vesting (sole and separate, joint tenants, etc.)
Must be recorded with county recorder and show recording information
Must reconcile with borrower names on loan application
For purchase transactions, must provide seller's vesting deed showing seller's ownership
reasons_needed: Final title policy vesting must reflect the name(s) of individual borrower(s)
Ownership must be verified to confirm borrower has legal interest in property
Vesting must align with loan application and title commitment
Manner of vesting affects loan documentation and signing requirements

specifications: Must identify creditor/servicer name and contact information
Must identify loan account number
Must show total payoff amount including all fees
Must show good-through date (minimum 30 days recommended)
Must identify subject property address or secured lien reference
Must include per diem interest amount if applicable
Must be dated within 30 days of closing
Must include any prepayment penalty amounts if applicable
Must show escrow balance to be refunded if applicable
reasons_needed: Existing liens must be paid at closing to ensure NQM first lien position
Payoff amount required to calculate funds to close
Good-through date must extend past anticipated closing date
Per diem required for accurate settlement calculations
Title must ensure first lien position without exception per NQMF guidelines

specifications: Must be completed within 10 business days of closing
Must verify current employment status
Must verify position/title
Must verify income if possible
Must include name and title of person contacted
Must include phone number used for verification
reasons_needed: Verbal VOE confirms employment status close to closing date
Ensures no changes to employment since written verification
document_requests.guideline_reference: Standard Full Documentation employment verification practice

specifications: Must include most recent 2 years of complete personal tax returns with all schedules
Must include Schedule C if borrower has sole proprietorship income
Must include Schedule E if borrower has rental property income
Must include Schedule K-1 if borrower has partnership or S-Corp income
Must be signed and dated
Must reconcile with W-2 reported wages
reasons_needed: Required if borrower has self-employment income, rental income, or other non-W2 income sources
Self-employed income qualification requires tax return analysis per guideline
Rental income calculation via FNMA Cash Flow Analysis requires Schedule E
document_requests.guideline_reference: SELF-EMPLOYMENT INCOME - Qualifying income is determined from Tax Returns

specifications: Must be prepared and signed by CPA, EA, or PTIN licensed tax preparer
Must be dated within 90 days of application date
Must cover year-to-date period
Must show gross revenue and itemized expenses
Preparer must attest they have prepared borrower's most recent tax returns
Expenses must be at least 20% of gross revenue
reasons_needed: P&L is used to determine stability of self-employed income per guideline
YTD trend analysis supports income continuity determination
Self-employment income qualification requires P&L in addition to tax returns
document_requests.guideline_reference: SELF-EMPLOYMENT INCOME - P&L is used to determine the stability of that income

specifications: Must be signed and dated by borrower and/or employer
Must explain reason for decline in income
Must provide supporting documentation for explanation
Must address whether decline is temporary or permanent
reasons_needed: Declining income sources require explanation to determine if income may be used for qualifying
If income shows consistent decline, it should not be considered stable or usable
When decline is explained and supported, most recent lower income must be used
document_requests.guideline_reference: DECLINING INCOME - signed, written explanation for the decline should be obtained
